payload.review_text: Verdict: One sufficiency blocker. The mechanism is sound and accurately grounded in diarizer/cli.py, config.py, session.py, and config/config.yaml (footgun claim verified; venv/Scripts/python.exe confirmed present; session.py stdlib-only confirmed). BLOCKER (S403, freshness): the PLAN eliminates the --config footgun but leaves the standing guidance documenting it intact and now-contradictory. CLAUDE.md 'Project operating rules' bullet ('Diarizer CLI: always pass --config config/config.yaml ... otherwise the HF token does not resolve and diarisation silently skips') and the user MEMORY note feedback_diarizer_config_flag.md both become false after execution. No Step and no files_touched entry updates them, though Context explicitly states the change 'removes the rule's reason to exist'. Fix: add a Step editing the CLAUDE.md Diarizer-CLI bullet (project-owned section, editable) to mark --config optional; surface the MEMORY note for operator update/retire; add CLAUDE.md to files_touched. Non-blocking warnings: (H8/S999) Step 6 names the README subsection by ambiguous prose rather than literal heading syntax; (S204) the --force reprocess path has no verification (skip path is covered by the human windows item, but --force override is untested). Non-blocking notes: launcher hardcodes 'venv' among three env dirs; stem-collision across extensions could mis-skip; three-artefact 'done' definition depends on existing conditional write paths (default txt/no-output drag path writes all three reliably). No real-judgement-call items to surface; the PLAN's listed 'judgement calls' are naming/mechanism choices resolvable autonomously per T15. Net: revise the 1 blocker and re-audit.

findings.location: CLAUDE.md 'Project operating rules'; MEMORY.md (feedback_diarizer_config_flag.md); PLAN frontmatter files_touched
findings.message: The PLAN makes config/config.yaml the default so --config is no longer required, but does not update the standing project rule that documents the removed footgun. CLAUDE.md's 'Diarizer CLI: always pass --config config/config.yaml ...' bullet and the MEMORY note feedback_diarizer_config_flag.md become false and directly contradict the new behaviour. Context acknowledges 'Making the project config the default removes the rule's reason to exist' yet no Step or files_touched entry addresses it.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Step editing the CLAUDE.md 'Diarizer CLI' bullet (it is in the project-owned section above the plan_foundry sentinel block, so editable) to state --config is now an optional override; surface the MEMORY note feedback_diarizer_config_flag.md for operator update/retire (it lives outside the repo); add CLAUDE.md to files_touched.

findings.message: Step 6 specifies the README deliverable section by ambiguous prose ('Add a short subsection (e.g. "Just run (drag-and-drop)")') rather than literal heading syntax. [H8 literal-heading discipline]
findings.suggested_fix: State the exact heading, e.g. 'MUST include a "## Just run (drag-and-drop)" heading in README.md'.

findings.message: The new --force override path has no verification. The second acceptance tests find_completed_session directly (not the _run wiring), and the human windows item exercises only the skip path, not reprocess-with---force. The guard is trivial (if not args.force), so warning not blocker.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the human item to also confirm --force reprocesses an already-completed file, or add an acceptance that exercises the _run skip/force branch.

findings.message: Launcher hardcodes venv\Scripts\python.exe. Confirmed present, but venv-old and venv-v2-test also exist; the assumption that 'venv' is the installed/correct environment is unstated.
findings.suggested_fix: Optionally note in Context that 'venv' is the canonical environment the launcher targets.

findings.message: Stem collision across extensions (foo.wav vs foo.m4a both -> foo_<timestamp>) could cause an incorrect skip. Low probability for this operator; note-level.
findings.suggested_fix: Optionally acknowledge the stem-based (extension-insensitive) matching limitation in Step 3's docstring or Context.
